title: 2 flavours of info()
body:
Before yesterday's PR was merged, I had written on my side the following function in system/


```
def info(api):
    """Returns information on the system and libpod configuration"""
    path = api.join("/info")
    response = api.request("GET", path)
    return json.loads(response.read())
```

upon seeing the PR coming in I first thought we had come up with the exact same thing independently, since master now has this

```
def get_info(api):
    """Returns information on the system and libpod configuration"""
    try:
        response = api.request("GET", "/info")
        return json.loads(str(response.read(), 'utf-8'))
    except errors.NotFoundError as e:
        _report_not_found(e, e.response)
```

but there is a difference indeed with the call to `api.join()` that inserts the base in the target url
the fact is that both these work fine, but return completely different information

this is probably obvious to people familiar with the libpod API and REST in general but that came as a bit of a surprise to me; I'd appreciate any reference that would shed light on that

I guess this issue resonates with #22 a little, in the sense that the naming of these functions should probably outline their respective scope somehow

comments.body:
The biggest difference here is the path evaluation.

In the first example the url evaluates to /v1.24/libpod/info
In the second example the url evalutes to /ingo

I can see the benefit of using the api.join method instead, however that will only work if libpod is going to keep multiple versions of the API around, by locking the API version we can create a consistent expectation for the user.

After looking at it I like the join method more myself, but that is predicated on keeping different versions of the API accessible.

If somebody from the libpod team can chime in as to what behaviour we should expect we can head the right direction.

agreed

the few functions that I wrote use the `api.join` thing because that's what the `images` functions were doing

in the particular instance of `info()` however I must stress that the 2 calls above return very different things, so I was curious that maybe they were 2 truly different things

in any case a little guidance would be most appreciated here :)

This issue affects other calls as well.

For example the uri /v1.23/libpod/system/df doesn't exist at all
but the uri /system/df works (but returns data the conflicts with the output of `podman system df`

This looks like intended behavior now that I've had a chance to look at the docs.

When omitting the /libpod/{version} from the path you are hitting the compatibility API endpoints.  My opinion is that for this SDK we shouldn't use those and should build it out so that all calls are structured to use the /libpod/{version} path (even if they are initially breaking).

See #24 for how I'm approaching this.

@bmarlow We intend to keep both the compatibile and libpod endpoints in the API.  The intent of this library is to provide a docker-py script access to the libpod endpoints with mInimal changes. A docker-py script will use the existing compatible endpoints, see [docker lIbpod tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/master/test/python/docker).

Example deltas:
* podman does not talk swarm
* docker-py does not talk pods
See https://pypi.org/project/podman-py/ API and results are now locked down.
